title: Union Minister of State Shri Tokhan Sahu meets heads of office in Champhai District; visits Mualkawi Solid Waste Management Centre
content:
120/2026-2027

Dated Champhai, 28th May 2026:Union Minister of State for Housing & Urban Affairs Pu Tokhan Sahu met with various heads of office today at the Circuit House Conference Hall, Champhai.

This programme started with a presentation from Sub-Deputy Commissioner Mr. Lalchhuanawma Ralte on various ongoing schemes and policies under Housing & Urban Affairs in Champhai such as Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U), Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana - National Urban Livelihood Mission (DAY-NULM) , Atul Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation (AMRUT) and many more. After the presentation, Minister Shri Tokhan Sahu interacted with various heads of office, asking them questions on their activities and the status of various key indicators in Champhai. The Minister spoke about various government schemes and policies that were relatively unknown but could be of great help for the people of Champhai, and urged the attendees to look into these schemes. He also urges government officials to keep up the good work and ensure that these schemes reach as many as possible.

After this meeting, Minister Shri Tokhan Sahu visited Mualkawi Solid Waste Management Centre, an ongoing project under AMRUT 2.0. This project is expected to be operational very shortly.

Attachment

title: Union Minister of State Pu Tokhan Sahu chuan Champhai pisa hotute nen inkawmna hun a hmang; Mualkawi Solid Waste Management Centre a tlawh
content:
741/2026-2027

Dated Champhai, 28th May 2026:Union Minister of State for Housing & Urban Affairs Pu Tokhan Sahu chuan vawiin khan Champhai Circuit House Conference Hall-ah Champhai pisa hotute nen inkawmna hun a hmang.

He hunah hian Champhai Sub-Deputy Commissioner Pu Lalchhuanawma Ralte chuan Housing & Urban Affairs hnuaia scheme Champhaia kalpui hrang hrang - Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U) te, Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana - National Urban Livelihood Mission (DAY-NULM) te, Atul Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation (AMRUT) te leh thil dang te sawifiahna hun a hmang a, hemi zawh hian Minister Pu Tokhan Sahu chuan pisa hotute chu kawmin zawhna te a zawt a, an harsatna te a ngaithla a ni. Minister chuan sawrkar scheme hrang hrang, mipuiin an la hriat hnem tawk loh, tangkai pui theih deuh tur si hi a awm teuh a, chung chu hriat tuma hmang tangkai turin mipuite a sawm a, chung scheme chu mi tam thei ang berin an hlawkpui theih nan sawrkar hnathawkte tang turin a fuih bawk a ni.

Inkawmna hun an hman zawh hian Minister Pu Tokhan Sahu chuan AMRUT 2.0 hnuaia sak mek Mualkawi Solid Waste Management Centre a tlawh a, a sak dan leh hnathawh kal dan te a endik a ni. He hmun hi bawlhhlawh paih nan hman tan tum mek a ni.

Attachment

title: Union Minister of State Shri Tokhan Sahu visits Champhai District
content:
119/2026-2027

Dated Champhai, 28th May 2026:Union Minister of State for Housing & Urban Affairs Shri Tokhan Sahu visited Champhai District today, as he was welcomed by heads of offices and leaders of various NGOs with a programme hosted by DC Office, Champhai at Circuit House, Champhai.

Minister Shri Tonkhan Sahu expressed gratitude for having the pleasure of visiting a beautiful district like Champhai, and thanked the organizers for putting together such a wonderful programme with entertaining performers. He reiterated the concept of unity and fraternity amidst our diverse cultural palette, and urged the attendees to embrace their cultural identity while forging friendships and brotherhoods with people from all over India to foster unity. He assured the attendees of the Hon'ble Prime Minister's commitment to development in the North East, as there are multiple projects and policies that are ongoing as well as upcoming that are currently benefiting people of the North East. Talking about the vision of One India, he urged the people of Champhai as well as the government servants to share in the beauty of the idea of One India - unified and strengthened under the roof of sustainable development, and commit to making the North East a better place for all.

The programme was hosted by Mr. Lalbiakfela, Addl. DC, Champhai. There were two dance performances from Champhai Vengthlang YMA Cultural Troupe and a vocal performance from Ms. Sylvia Malsawmpuii. Mr. Ngulzathuama, District Urban Development Officer, Champhai closed the programme with a vote of thanks.

After the programme, Minister Shri Tokhan Sahu interacted with beneficiaries under Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U) and Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana - National Urban Livelihood Mission (DAY-NULM).

Attachment

title: Union Minister of State Pu Tokhan Sahu chuan Champhai District a tlawh
content:
740/2026-2027

Dated Champhai, 28th May 2026:Union Minister of State for Housing & Urban Affairs Pu Tokhan Sahu chuan vawiin khan Champhai a tlawh a, DC Office buatsaihin Champhai Circuit House-ah sawrkar hotute leh khawtlang hruaitute nen hun a hmang.

Minister Pu Tokhan Sahu chuan Champhai hmun mawi tak a rawn thleng thei leh hetiang hun tha leh hlimawm tak an lo buatsaih hi lawmawm a tih hle thu a sawi a, inpumkhatna pawimawh zia sawiin India ramah hian hman atang tawhin inpumkhatna pawimawh zia kan sawi tawh a, kan hnam leh nihna hi hluta hrechiang chunga India ram mi dang te nena inpumkhatna vawng nung turin a chah. Tun sawrkar hian North East hi a ngai pawimawh em em a, hmalakna hrang hrang a awm a, hna pawh thahnem tak thawh tawh a ni tih a sawi a, One India siam hi an tum ber a ni tih sawiin Prime Minister thiltum a hlawhtlin theih nan heta sawrkar hnathawkte tha taka thawk turin a sawm a, mipui pawn he One India - India inpumkhatna siam thei turin tan la zel turin a chah bawk a ni.

A lawmna hun hi Addl. DC Pu Lalbiakfela'n a kaihruai a, Champhai Vengthlang Cultural Troupe te'n lam an hmuh a, intihhlimna hun hman a nih hnuah District Urban Development Officer (DUDO) Ngulzathuama'n lawmthu sawiin hun a khar a ni.

Programme zawh hian Minister Pu Tokhan Sahu chuan Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U) leh Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana - National Urban Livelihood Mission (DAY-NULM) hnuaia hamthatna dawngtute nen inkawmna hun a hmang a ni.

Attachment

title: KVIC Records Historic Growth; Sales Cross Rs 1.87 Lakh Crore in 2025-26
content:
118/2026-2027

The Khadi and Village Industries Commission (KVIC) under the Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises has achieved record-breaking growth during the financial year 2025-26, with total sales of Khadi and Village Industries products crossing Rs 1.87 lakh crore for the first time.

Releasing the provisional data for 2025-26 at Gandhi Darshan, Rajghat in New Delhi, KVIC Chairman Shri Manoj Kumar said the sector has witnessed unprecedented progress under the leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi through initiatives such as “Aatmanirbhar Bharat”, “Vocal for Local”, and “Local to Global”.

According to the provisional figures, sales of Khadi and Village Industries products rose to Rs 1,87,105 crore in 2025-26 from Rs 31,154 crore in 2013-14, registering a growth of 501 percent over the last 12 years. Production also increased from Rs 26,109 crore to Rs 1,25,296 crore during the same period, recording a growth of 380 percent.

The KVIC Chairman stated that the sector’s expansion reflects the growing entrepreneurship and self-reliance in rural India. He added that the achievements were made possible through the guidance of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, inspiration from Mahatma Gandhi, and the efforts of millions of artisans across the country.

The Khadi textiles sector also registered remarkable growth. Production increased from Rs 811 crore in 2013-14 to Rs 3,974 crore in 2025-26, while sales rose from Rs 1,081 crore to Rs 7,869 crore.

Similarly, the Village Industries sector saw production rise from Rs 25,298 crore to Rs 1,21,322 crore, while sales increased from Rs 30,073 crore to Rs 1,79,236 crore during the period.

KVIC also achieved significant progress in employment generation. Employment linked to Khadi and Village Industries activities increased from 1.30 crore people in 2013-14 to 2.04 crore in 2025-26, marking a growth of 56 percent.

Under the Prime Minister’s Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP), 66,494 new units were established during 2025-26 with margin money subsidy of Rs 2,457 crore disbursed against loans amounting to Rs 7,375 crore. These units generated employment opportunities for more than 7.31 lakh people.

KVIC further stated that women empowerment remains a major focus area. During 2025-26, over 79,000 trainees received training under various KVIC programmes, of which nearly 59 percent were women. In addition, 28,180 women entrepreneurs established business units under PMEGP during the year, generating employment for more than 3 lakh women.

The Commission also highlighted its efforts under the Gramodyog Vikas Yojana, through which lakhs of machines, toolkits, and equipment have been distributed to artisans and rural entrepreneurs to strengthen self-employment and promote rural industries.

KVIC Chairman Shri Manoj Kumar said the achievements of the Khadi and Village Industries sector are contributing significantly towards the vision of “Viksit Bharat@2047” while strengthening India’s rural economy and self-reliance.

title: Health Minister Pi Lalrinpuii’n MGNSA Conference a hmanpui
content:
739/2026-2027

Aizawl, the 28th May, 2026:Vawiin khan Health Minister Pi Lalrinpuiii chuan Mizoram Government Nursing Service Association (MGNSA) Conference vawi-23na chu Aizawl Civil Hospital Conference Hall-ah a hmanpui. Kumin MGNSA Conference thupui atan Soft Skill for Nurses tih chu hman a ni.

Pi Lalrinpuii chuan Nurse hna hi hna naran a ni lova; mi bik, inpekna nasa tak nei ten taimakna, dawhtheihna leh inpumpekna nasa tak nena thawh ngai a ni, a ti. Mizoram sorkar chuan Nurse te pawimawhzia hriain, an indaihlohnate phuhru turin kum 2025 April thlaa Mizoram Universal Health Care Scheme tlangzarh a nih dawn khan Nurse 300 chuang lak an ni a, hei bakah hian a nghetin Nursing Officer sawm (10) lak an ni tih a sawi a. Health & Family Welfare hnuaiah contract-in Nurse 40 lak an ni leh dawn a, Senior Nursing Officer sawm (10) pawh lak thuai a ni dawn, Minister chuan a ti. Nursing School lamah Tutor post 9, leh Warden post 1 atan MPSC written exam result nghah mek a ni a.  Hei bakah hian Nursing College lam atan Assistant Professor post 5 advertisement nghah mek a ni bawk tih sawiin Pi Lalrinpuii chuan vawi leh khatah an indaihlohnate phuhru thei lo mahse, hmasawn zel turin Mizoram Sawrkar chuan hmalak chhunzawm zel a tum a ni, a ti.

Aizawl Civil Hospital tichangtlung tura sawrkar hmalakna hrang hrang sawiin Cath lab dah thar a ni a, MRI khawl bakah bungraw chi hrang hrang dah a ni a, mipui chhiah tlingkhawm – CESS atangin Artroscope khawl thar cheng nuai 120 lei a ni a, kal thlak (kidney transplant) pawh tih tan thuai tum niin inzaina bungrua lei chhoh mek a ni a, thawktute pawh a bika training turin Kolkata tirh an ni tawh, a ti a. Current awm loh zauh zauh changa Civil Hospital te an buai lohna turin VIP line zamna tur pawisa leh Oxygen Pump khawl thra man tur pawh sawrkarin a pe fel tawh tih a sawi bawk.

Pi Lalrinpuii chuan ZMCH lamah pawh Cardio-thorasic surgery ti turin an inbuatsaih fel tawh a, thawktute training neiin PGI Chandigarh lamah an kal tawh bawk a; CT Scan thar hawn fel niin, Siaha leh Champhai ah CT Scan dah thar a ni bawk a; Oxygen Plant thar neihna tur sum Sawrkarin a pe tawh a; Mamit District Hospital building thar pawh luahluh mai tura peihfel tawh niin, Serchhip, Kolasib leh Hnahthial District Hospital pawh sak mek a ni, a ti.

Conference hmang tur hian Directorate of Nursing hotute leh Mizoram hmun hrang hrang atangin MGNSA member te an kal khawm a. National Florence Nightingale Award dawng mi pahnih (2) Pi V.Lalhmangaihi, Nursing Officer leh Pi H.Mankimi, Senior Nursing Officer te bakah hruaitu chawimawina Pu Lalhmingmawia Thangluah, Nursing Officer hnenah hlan a ni.

MGNSA President chuan Pu H.S. Lalremtluanga, thuchah a sawi a. Kum 2024 – 2026 MGNSA Conference inkara an member boral pakhat (1) sunna hun an hmang a. Pension sawmnga pakhat (51) an thlah a. An Vice President Pu Zoramchhana’n lawmthu sawiin hun a khar a ni.

Attachment

title: NEC Spends ₹339.06 Crore on Development Projects in Mizoram During 15th Finance Commission Period
content:
117/2026-2027

28th May, 2026:The North Eastern Council (NEC) has continued to strengthen its developmental engagement in Mizoram through major investments in healthcare, education, connectivity, tourism, agriculture, and livelihood sectors during the 15th Finance Commission (2022–26) period.

According to an official press release, Mizoram received a cumulative expenditure of ₹339.06 crore under the Schemes of NEC (SoNEC) and NESIDS-Roads programmes during the period.

Under the SoNEC programme, NEC spent ₹123.83 crore on various developmental initiatives across the state. The projects covered medical and veterinary facilities, market infrastructure, educational institutions, cultural centres, sports infrastructure, and community development works.

Special emphasis was also given to agriculture and allied sectors through promotion of wet rice cultivation, potato seed production, piggery, poultry, fisheries, arecanut cultivation, passion fruit processing, and livestock development.

The NEC further supported preservation and promotion of Mizoram’s cultural and natural heritage through the establishment of cultural heritage centres, tourism circuits, ecological parks, and other cultural infrastructure aimed at boosting eco-tourism and cultural tourism in the state.

During the 15th Finance Commission period, six road projects worth ₹277.99 crore were implemented in Mizoram under the NESIDS-Roads programme, out of which two projects valued at ₹25.19 crore have already been completed.

The Council also highlighted the upgradation of the Saiha-Lungbun-Tlangnuam-Haka Road, which is expected to improve connectivity between Mizoram’s southern districts and the Myanmar border, supporting the broader objectives of India’s Act East Policy.

NEC also expanded its youth and scientific outreach initiatives through programmes such as the Ashtalakshmi Youth Exchange Programme and NE-SPARKS during 2025–26.

Under the Ashtalakshmi Youth Exchange Programme, 581 students from across the country visited premier institutions in the Northeast, including Mizoram University, which hosted 44 students from Andaman & Nicobar Islands and Lakshadweep in January 2026.

Meanwhile, under NE-SPARKS (North East Students’ Programme for Awareness, Reach and Knowledge on Space), 99 students from Mizoram were among 786 students from the Northeastern states who visited the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) during 2025–26 to promote scientific awareness and innovation.

The NEC also released funds during April 2026 for ongoing projects in Mizoram, including livestock rearing support in the Sinlung Hills Council area, establishment of a State Veterinary Information and Resource Centre, construction of a market building at West Phaileng in Mamit district, and tourism development at Vangchhia in Champhai district under the initiative “Our Heritage, Our Treasure.”

In addition, NEC released ₹20.09 crore under NESIDS (Roads) during April 2026 for ongoing road projects, including the construction of the Pukzing–Silsury Road and Tualpui–Champhai Road in Mizoram.

Attachment

title: 15th Finance Commission (2022–26) hun chhungin NEC-in Mizoram tan cheng vaibelchhe 339.06 a seng
content:
738/2026-2027

28th May, 2026:North Eastern Council (NEC) chuan 15th Finance Commission (2022–26) hun chhungin Mizoram-ah healthcare, zirna, inkalpawhna kawngpui, tourism, agriculture leh mamawh hrang hrangah hmasawnna hnalian tam tak a thawk.

NEC Official press release-in a tarlan danin Schemes of NEC (SoNEC) leh NESIDS-Roads programme hnuaiah Mizoram-ah cheng vaibelchhe 339.06 crore sen a ni.

SoNEC programme hnuaiah hian NEC-in cheng vaibelchhe 123.83 sengin medical leh veterinary, bazar, zirna in, cultural centre, infiamna leh khawtlang mamawh hmasawnna ruhrel pawimawh tak tak sak thar leh thuam that hna a thawk a. Kuthnathawktute tan leilet siam, alu (potato) thar chhuah, vawk leh ar vulh, sangha dil siam, kuhva hmun siam, sapthei sawngbawl leh ranvulh kawnga hmasawnna atan hma nasa takin a la bawk.

Pi leh pute sul hnu, hmun hlui leh Mizo culture humhalhna turin cultural heritage centre, tourism circuit, ecological park leh cultural infrastructure dangte siam a ni a. Heng ruhrel din leh cheithat te hi eco-tourism leh cultural tourism hmasawnna atan pawh tangkai hle tura ngaih a ni.

NESIDS-Roads programme hnuaiah 15th Finance Commission hun chhungin Mizoram-ah cheng vaibelchhe 277.99 man hu road project paruk (6) kalpui a ni a. Chung zinga project pahnih, cheng vaibelchhe 25.19 senna chu zawh fel tawh a ni.

NEC chuan Saiha-Lungbun-Tlangnuam-Haka kawngpui siamthat hna pawh a thawk a. Hei hian Mizoram chhim lam district te leh Myanmar ramri inkara inkalpawhna tha zawk a siam bakah, India Sawrkar Act East Policy tihhlawhtlinna kailawn pawimawh tak a ni.

Ashtalakshmi Youth Exchange Programme hnuaiah India ram hmun hrang hrang atangin zirlai 581 te chuan Northeast-a Zirna In pawimawhte an tlawh a, January 2026 khan Andaman & Nicobar Islands leh Lakshadweep atangin zirlai 44 te chuan Mizoram University an tlawh a ni.

NE-SPARKS (North East Students’ Programme for Awareness, Reach and Knowledge on Space) programme hnuaiah North East State hrang hrang atangin zirlai 786 te chuan Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) an tlawh a, an zingah hian Mizoram atangin zirlai 99 (mipa 45 leh hmeichhia 54) an tel an ni.

April 2026-ah khan NEC chuan Sinlung Hills Council area-a khawsak harsa zawk te tan ranvulh tanpuina a pe a, State Veterinary Information and Resource Centre a din a, West Phaileng-ah market building leh Champhai district-a Vangchhia-ah hmasawnna ruhrel din nan sum a pechhuak (release) bawk a ni.

Hei bakah hian NESIDS (Roads) hnuaiah April 2026 khan cheng vaibelchhe 20.09 release a ni a. Hei hi kawngpui sial leh cheithat hna kalpui mek te bakah Pukzing–Silsury kawngpui leh Tualpui–Champhai kawngpui siam hna thawh nana hman tur a ni.

Attachment
